rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The administrator made essentially the same allegation about why the estate cannot close in her second accounting petition filed on 11/19/24. Does she have proof of the communication from ICE re its timeline? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/7/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL JEAN DAMM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 05/06/2025): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. The captain must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". See LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) No DE-120 form (notice of hearing with proof of service) is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) A copy of the lost will was not attached to the 11/07/2023 order for probate. A drop-off ex parte petition must be filed to request an amended order for probate with a copy of the lost will attached. See Probate Code section 8223 & LRSF 14.20. 4) A waiver of account signed by petitioner, specifying his capacity as trustee of the devisee trust, must be filed. The waiver must also state that petitioner has accepted the trust. See section 10954(b)(4). 5) The estate cash on hand as of a date certain must be stated. See CRC 7.651(b)(1). 6) A specific amount of net cash distributable to the devisee trust as of a date certain (after deducting the attorney's statutory fees and costs from the estate cash on hand) must be specified. See LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(a). 7) Proof of compliance with section 10533(a)(1) or (2) must be filed to show the entire estate cash is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is otherwise held in the form of authorized investment(s). 8) The date of the devisee trust as the proposed sole distributee set forth in the petition does not match the date of the trust specified in the will. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDDA ELIZABETH BODDIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Distribution cannot be made until a certified copy of Letters certified within 60 days is filed, issued to the personal representative of the deceased heir's estate. [LRSF 14.35 G.] (2) A petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (3) Neither the administrator's nor the attorney's costs advanced were sufficiently itemized, showing the DATE the cost was incurred or paid, the PAYEE, the description, and the amount. (The filing fee for the initial petition was $435, not $498.) (4) Why was there airfare for the administrator to deal with the vehicle when both the administrator and the decedent reside or resided in San Francisco? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/7/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The accounting was waived, so the incomplete accounting attached as Attachment 1 to the petition will be disregarded. (No dates or fully itemized entries.)) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL JOHN MEEHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/30/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney with the following issues: 1) Probate Code 9202(b) has been revised to require notice even if heirs/beneficiaries have been previously confined. Please provide revised allegation re: California Victim Compensation Board. 2) Summary of Account includes "stock value" change in the amount of $10,226.59. If this is a loss on sale, it must be included in the fee base. If this represents an unrealized loss, the assets on hand must specify the actual securities that are on hand. 3) The proposed distribution must be specific and after deducting the cash on hand by all the fees, costs, and reserve requested. If there are securities, the specific proposed distribution of securities is also needed. 4) Petitioner alleges that there have been preliminary distributions. Please provide the statutory authority and file all receipts. If any of the specific distributions listed in the will have not been made, an explanation will be needed. 5) A revised proposed order must be filed after the issues above are addressed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL OYKHERMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner on 05/19/2025): 1) No proof of publication is on file. 2) Please file a declaration attaching a copy of the power of attorney signed by Larisa Baytman. The declaration must be signed with the following language required by CCP section 2015.5 added: "I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the foregoing is true and correct." The declaration must state that the attached document is a true and correct copy of the power of attorney. 3) If Larisa Baytman is a citizen of Russia, notice must be given to an appropriate Russian Consulate (or the Embassy if there is no Consulate) in the United States, as required by Probate Code section 8113. Notice must be given by mailing a copy of a new DE-121 form showing the next hearing date, together with the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6). The date of mailing must be at least 15 days before the hearing. A new DE-121 form must make the following corrections: i) The county (San Francisco) must be specified in the caption where the Court's address is shown. ii) The decedent's name, instead of petitioner's, must be stated in the caption where the document title is shown. 4) As petitioner is a nominee of the sole beneficiary who is not a US resident: i) Each factor of section 8465(d) must be addressed. Specifically, please file a declaration in response to the following: A) Does petitioner have a conflict of interest with Larisa Baytman or any other interested party? B) Did petitioner have a business relationship with the decedent or his family before the decedent's death? C) Is petitioner engaged in or acting on behalf of any individual, business, or other entity that solicits heirs to obtain the person's nomination for appointment as administrator? D) Has petitioner been appointed as personal representative in any other estate? ii) Section 8465(e) requires petitioner to give a full bond ($185,000), absent good cause. A bond waiver by the beneficiary does not constitute good cause under section 8465(e). Please file a declaration in response to the following: A) Is petitioner bondable for $185,000 if ordered by the Court? B) If petitioner is unable or unwilling to give a bond, is petitioner amenable to an order requiring petitioner to obtain court approval before taking possession of money or other property of the decedent (see item 5d of the proposed order)? For money, a blocked account must be opened. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUSAN SO-PIK LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) If Betty Lee resides in Alameda County, then item 8 of the petition is false and notice to her is defective. [CRC 7.51(c).] (2) Item 3d of the petition is incomplete: only the estimate of the personal property and real property are set forth without any income or encumbrance or subtotal or total. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAMELA ANN KLOTOVICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/25. (No earlier availability.) The lost will has apparently still not been filed. No will file has been created and provided to the assigned examiner as of 8/20/25. The Proof of Holographic Instrument filed on 7/22/25 does not have a typewritten copy attached to it. Whose son is Kenneth M. Klotovich? The decedent's letter dated 6/28/15 refers to a will that her attorney has. Did that will predate this lost holographic will? Deadline is 9/30/25 to provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address the issues to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ILENE RENEE DICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of filing the Inventory and Appraisal is set for 12/30/25. If the I&A is not filed by 12/26/25, an appearance will be required on 12/30/25. Status of closing the estate will be set for 3/9/2027. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 3/9/2027. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed.
